summary.title: Dubai Marina Residential Market — Q2 2026
summary.narrative: Dubai Marina remains one of Dubai's most liquid and active residential investment submarkets, driven by strong expat demand from the financial, tech, and professional services sectors. Rents have grown +7.2% year-on-year in the Marina, supported by limited new supply and continued population growth. Marina Gate I by Select Group is positioned at the premium end of the Marina submarket — offering full hotel-style amenities and direct marina frontage. The subject unit has demonstrated 15% appreciation since 2022 and yields a strong 5.27% net cap rate.
keyThemes: Dubai Marina vacancy at 4.8% — near record low; rental growth +7.2% YoY
Marina Gate I maintains premium positioning due to Select Group amenities package
AED/USD peg at 3.6725 — no currency risk for USD trust reporting
Dubai luxury residential global demand continues from Russian, European, and Asian HNW buyers
New supply pipeline of ~8,000 units in Marina over 2025–2027 will moderate but not reverse growth

strengths: Marina Gate I is a premium Select Group development — full hotel amenities, direct marina access
Floor 23 — good views, above lower-floor noise; attractive to corporate tenants
2019-built — relatively modern vs. Marina Gate comparables from 2000s
AED 138K/yr rent at 5.27% cap rate — well-positioned on yield basis
weaknesses: 2BR (106 sqm) — increasingly competitive segment as supply grows
Service charge AED 20,600/yr reduces net yield vs. comparable buildings
BVI holding structure adds USD 2,800/yr maintenance cost
opportunities: Rental upside on renewal to AED 143K–148K (+4–7%) given current Dubai Marina comps
Marina Gate I resale market active — exit optionality at AED 2.7–2.9M achievable
threats: New 2BR supply in Dubai Marina over 2025–2027 could moderate rent growth
UAE corporate tax changes could affect BVI holding structure attractiveness

comparables.notes: Adjacent tower; newer (2021 build); slightly higher per sqm
Older building; lower specification; higher cap rate compensates
Premium location; slight premium to subject; strong recent appreciation

rentComparables.amenities: Marina frontage, infinity pool, private beach, gym
Similar Select Group amenities; newer build

marketRisks.title: New Supply Moderation (2025–2027)
marketRisks.description: ~8,000 Marina units in pipeline over next 2 years; well-managed absorption expected; Marina Gate I premium insulates.

marketRisks.title: Global Capital Cycle Risk
marketRisks.description: Any global risk-off event could reduce Dubai ultra-luxury investor demand; Marina 2BR segment broader-based and more resilient.

marketOpportunities.title: Rental Uplift on Renewal (+7%)
marketOpportunities.description: Dubai Marina rental growth of 7.2% YoY supports AED 143K–148K target on April 2027 renewal — $3,700–$5,400 additional annual income.

marketOpportunities.title: Capital Appreciation Trend Continues
marketOpportunities.description: Marina Gate I comparable transactions suggesting AED 2.75–2.90M range — upside of AED 130K–280K from current AED 2.62M assessed value.

marketImplication.title: Strong Performer — Hold and Optimise Rental on Renewal
marketImplication.body: Marina Gate I Unit 2304 is the portfolio's strongest-yielding asset at 5.27% net cap rate, and with 15% appreciation since 2022, demonstrates both income and capital return. The recommended strategy is hold-and-let: renew the tenancy in April 2027 at AED 143K–148K (+4–7%), maintaining the existing quality corporate tenant base. The Dubai Marina market remains undersupplied relative to demand, and Marina Gate I's premium amenities provide durable competitive advantage. No disposal recommendation — this is the portfolio's benchmark income asset.
